questions.question: A cell with E°cell > 0 is ______.

options.A: Spontaneous
options.B: Non-spontaneous
options.C: Equilibrium

questions.question: Kohlrausch Law application: It helps determine ______ for weak electrolytes.

options.A: Λm° (limiting molar conductivity)
options.B: Degree of dissociation
options.C: Dissociation constant (Ka)

questions.question: Nernst equation at 298K: E = E° - (0.0591/n) log ______.

options.A: Q (reaction quotient)
options.B: K (equilibrium constant)
options.C: Mass

questions.question: E° value for Li+/Li is -3.05 V. This indicates Li is a ______.

options.A: Strong oxidant
options.B: Strong reductant
options.C: Noble metal

questions.question: Strictly calculate the correct statement regarding Electrochemistry Redox: According to Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions, the limiting molar conductivity of an electrolyte is equal to: [Variant 2-1]

options.A: The sum of the individual contributions of the anion and cation of the electrolyte
options.B: The product of the specific conductivities
options.C: The difference between cation and anion mobilities

options.B: Methane
options.C: Hydrogen
